# Supervision

A native macOS code-review companion for LLM-driven development. You review the
working-tree diff and leave comments anchored to specific lines; a coding agent
reads those comments and applies them. Comments live in
`.supervision/comments.json` at the repo root.

## Architecture

- Desktop app built on Electrobun (NOT Electron — do not use Electron APIs).
  Uses the system WebView, no bundled Chromium.
- `src/bun/` — main process (Bun): git, file watcher, RPC bridge, comment
  store, app menu, config. HMR does NOT reload this process; restart `dev`
  after editing.
- `src/mainview/` — React UI (diff pane, comments panel, toolbar, settings).
  `src/mainview/web/` is web mode: the full app against an in-memory backend for
  tests, no Electrobun. Nothing under `web/` ships in the packaged app.
- `src/shared/` — types shared across the process boundary.
- `skills/supervision/SKILL.md` — the `/supervision` agent skill that applies
  review comments.

## Conventions

- Run shell commands through devbox: `devbox run -- bun run <script>`.
- `docs/specs/` is the source of truth for UI behavior. Every bug fix starts
  with a failing spec-tagged test (`AREA-N` id in the test title); see
  `docs/specs/README.md`. `scripts/check-spec-coverage.ts` enforces coverage.
- Full gate: `devbox run -- bun run test` (typecheck + unit + spec coverage +
  e2e). Format/lint with Biome: `devbox run -- bun run check`.

## The comment contract

`.supervision/comments.json` holds review comments anchored to a file + line,
each with a reply thread. The `/supervision` skill reads open comments, makes
the change, and replies inline in the JSON. See `skills/supervision/SKILL.md`
for the full schema and workflow.

## Electrobun reference

- API reference: https://blackboard.sh/electrobun/llms.txt
- Docs: https://blackboard.sh/electrobun/docs/
- Imports: main process `electrobun/bun`, browser context `electrobun/view`.
- Bundled assets load via `views://` URLs, configured in `electrobun.config.ts`.
